reasoning: The weekly series WGS1YR had a value of 3.95 on the week ending Friday, April 11. Due to sudden volatile sell-offs in the bond market on April 8-11 driven by tariff concerns, the daily 1-year yield (DGS1) closed at 4.04 on April 11. Over the next week (resolving April 20), this recent spike means the weekly average will almost certainly reset significantly higher than 3.95, yielding a very high probability for the first resolution date. For May 13, inflationary pressure from the tariffs likely keeps yields elevated, but some reversion to the trend limits confidence relative to April. For July and October, probability regresses smoothly toward 0.5 due to uncertainty regarding the Fed's longer-term rate cut trajectory (markets still price in 1-2 cuts for 2025, which could drag the 1-year yield back below 3.95 later in the year if recessionary pressures dominate).
